summary: Completed comprehensive testing of emotional voice intelligence implementation in Next.js voice assistant app. All 179 vitest tests pass including 12 new emotion detection tests. Verified all code implementations match specifications exactly.
passed_tests: All 12 emotion detection tests pass (detectEmotionFromAudio and getEmotionAdaptation functions)
All 4 useEmotionDetector hook tests pass (smoothing logic, confidence handling, reset functionality)
Full vitest suite passes: 179 tests across 16 files
Emotion types properly exported (EmotionState, EmotionProfile, EmotionAdaptation)
Pure client-side emotion detector with zero external imports
Correct emotion mappings: all zeros → neutral, high energy + fast + high pitch → excited, low energy + slow + low pitch → fatigued
Proper adaptation configurations: stressed → 300 tokens/0.8 stability, neutral → empty suffix, confident → 1000 tokens
Smoothing logic working: same emotion 2x → confirmed, different → no flicker, low confidence → neutral adaptation
Updated validation schemas with optional TTS and chat parameters
Gemini request builder accepts optional maxOutputTokens defaulting to 600
TTS route passes stability, similarityBoost, style parameters correctly
Chat route reads maxOutputTokens and memories from body and passes to Gemini
Voice state machine properly integrates emotion detection with audio snapshots and analysis
StatusDisplay shows 6px emotion indicator dot during recording with correct color mapping
Chat page passes currentEmotion to StatusDisplay component
success_percentage: 100% - All emotion detection functionality working correctly, all tests passing
